title: pyrus cydonia subsp. maliformis
intro: Pyrus cydonia subsp. maliformis, also known as the quince, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and Asia. The quince plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and relieving symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery, due to its high content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of quince include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Quince can be prepared as a tea, syrup, or jam, and is also used in traditional medicine as a dried fruit or in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
